data.title: Trump assures investors that his policies 'never change'
data.description:
President Donald Trump sought to assure investors on Friday morning that the United States is still a good place to invest.

"TO THE MANY INVESTORS COMING INTO THE UNITED STATES AND INVESTING MASSIVE AMOUNTS OF MONEY, MY POLICIES WILL NEVER CHANGE. THIS IS A GREAT TIME TO GET RICH, RICHER THAN EVER BEFORE!!!" he wrote on a social media post.

Still, equity markets continue to bleed red, with the Nasdaq dropping 4.3%, S&P 500 falling 3.2%, and the Dow off 2.8% in early Friday trading. The 10-year Treasury yield sank below the 4% mark to 3.94%. The Dollar Index, meanwhile, edged up 0.1%.

He also touted the March jobs numbers, which came in stronger than expected, adding that his policies are already working. "HANG TOUGH, WE CAN’T LOSE!!!," he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: PayPal adds Chainlink, Solana cryptocurrencies to its platforms
data.description:
PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) (NEOE:PYPL:CA) announced on Friday the addition of two cryptocurrencies - Chainlink (LINK-USD) and Solana (SOL-USD) - to its PayPal and Venmo platforms.

Shares were trading -3.73% pre-market at $59.41.

PayPal and Venmo users can buy, hold, sell, and transfer the two cryptocurrencies directly in their respective accounts.

The announcement comes as cryptocurrency-linked stocks face downward pressure on the back of President Donald Trump's new import tariffs.

"Since we initially made cryptocurrencies available on PayPal and Venmo, we've been listening to our users about what they want to do with crypto on our platforms. One piece of feedback we've heard is to make additional tokens available that align with our mission of revolutionizing payments," said May Zabaneh, vice president of product, blockchain, crypto, and digital currencies, at the digital payments company.

"Offering more tokens on PayPal and Venmo provides users with greater flexibility, choice, and access to digital currencies. This allows for more options to buy, send or spend within our trusted PayPal and Venmo wallets," added Zabaneh.

data.title: Meta likely to release Llama 4 model later this month: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) is likely to release the next large language model in the Llama model family, Llama 4, later this month, The Information reported.

The release of Llama 4 has been pushed back at least twice due to performance issues, with underperformance in reasoning and math tasks, the news outlet added, citing two people familiar with the matter. There are also concerns that Llama 4 is less capable than OpenAI's models in having humanlike voice conversations, the people added.

Meta unveiled its most recent Llama model, Llama 3.3, in December. 

Several of Meta's competitors have released new versions of their large language models in recent weeks, including Google (GOOGL) (GOOGL), which released Gemini 2.5 on March 25. 

Meta is also discussing offering a Llama API for businesses as part of an internal program known, Llama X. This could let companies use Llama more easily in their own software and provide Meta with added revenue, the news outlet added, citing three people familiar with the matter.

Morgan Stanley said in March that the release of Llama 4 could come in the Spring of 2025. The company is also gearing up for its LlamaCon developer event next week, where it is expected to provide updates on the Llama family of models, along with other AI innovations,

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg said in October 2024 that the company's upcoming models are “being trained on a cluster that is bigger than 100,000 [Nvidia] H100s or bigger than anything that I've seen reported for what others are doing.”

Last month, Meta announced that its Llama artificial intelligence models have been downloaded more than 1B times.

data.title: Wall Street rout continues, but strong jobs report gives some relief; Powell in focus
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Friday slumped at the open, extending their losses from the previous session which was the worst day for Wall Street since 2020. Sentiment continues to be hammered by President Donald Trump's imposition of the steepest American trade barriers in over a century.

Retaliation from other countries to the sweeping U.S. reciprocal tariffs was kicked off by China, which slapped a 34% tariff on U.S. imports.

Market participants did receive a reprieve on Friday in the form of a surprisingly strong jobs report. The focus now turns to an upcoming speech by Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell at 1125 ET.

Minutes after the open, the S&P 500 (SP500) was -2.5%. The benchmark index is now -14.3% from its most recent record close. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2.7%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -2.2%.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields slumped as traders flocked to the safety of bonds. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 10 basis points to 3.94%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 11 basis points to 3.57%.

Two days after U.S. President Donald Trump announced sweeping tariffs against several countries, China has retaliated with 34% tariffs on all U.S. imports from April 10, according to the country’s official Xinhua News Agency.

The economic calendar grabbed attention after nonfarm payrolls blew past expectations in March. The unemployment rate edged up.

"Friday's jobs report shows that the economy is still creating jobs at a robust pace even with the tariff uncertainty and Federal job cuts, and job growth is arguably the most important economic indicator," Glen Smith, chief investment officer at GDS Wealth Management, said. However, he cautioned that the data was "backward looking and doesn't say anything about how employers might fare over the coming months."

"Federal Reserve Chair Powell has a key opportunity to calm markets in his speech Friday, and that is what investors are hoping for. Assurance from the central bank that they will step in to keep prices stable and employment continuing is something investors are eager to be reminded about," Smith added. 

U.S. stocks were mauled Thursday, with the S&P 500 closing in a correction as President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs stoked fears of a global trade war. The S&P 500 closed at -4.8%, finishing its worst session since June 2020.

The selloff occurred after Trump late Wednesday detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%.

data.title: Sarepta, Roche seek to resume Elevidys trials
data.description:
Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) announced Friday that the company and its partner Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) expect to provide EU officials with data in support of their gene therapy Elevidys, seeking an end to a temporary clinical hold imposed on several studies for the drug.

Elevidys, approved in the U.S. to treat the genetic disorder Duchenne muscular dystrophy, is currently the subject of a temporary clinical hold imposed on several of its trials following a patient death linked to the drug in March.

Sarepta (NASDAQ:SRPT) said that the studies’ independent data monitoring committee has determined that the trials can be continued without modifying their protocols, as Elevidys has continued to demonstrate a favorable benefit-risk profile.

As requested by the EU regulators, the companies intend to submit the data within a week in response to the temporary halt. The data submission is expected to follow a regulatory process, after which the EU officials are expected to decide on lifting the temporary halt, the company added.

data.title: Booking Holdings stands out as a leader in lodging - BTIG
data.description:
While the current debate centers on the potential for a macro-driven U.S. slowdown that will disproportionally impact the travel industry, BTIG upgrades Booking Holdings (NASDAQ:BKNG) to Buy labeling the company a “global leader in lodging with an excellent track record and best-in-class management.”

To frame his investment case with a Buy recommendation, BTIG’s Jake Fuller cites Bookings’ (NASDAQ:BKNG) attractive margins, free cash flow conversion at ~90%, “excellent” balance sheet with $1B+ of net cash, and confidence that the company can sustain mid-teems EPS growth. Additionally, the current valuation against all that is “not particularly challenging.”

As the company pushes beyond traditional lodging, Fuller sees Booking (NASDAQ:BKNG) able to exceed high-single-digit topline and mid-teens EPS growth algorithms as it pursues these “under-appreciated opportunities.”

For Q1, Fuller expects Booking (BKNG) to report better-than-guided results, with room nights book reaching +8% or better (vs 5%-7% guidance). And with foreign exchange now serving as a tailwind instead of a headwind, Booking (BKNG) is likely to guide for an upbeat Q2.

Seeking Alpha authors and Wall Street analysts agree with Fuller's assessment of Booking (BKNG), also rating the stock as a Buy. Seeking Alpha's Quant rating, however, views Booking (BKNG) as a Hold with a Quant score of 3.28 out of 5.

Booking (BKNG) reports Q1 results after the market close on April 29.

Booking (BKNG) shares are down 1.8% into Friday’s open in sympathy with a broad market sell-off, outperforming rivals Airbnb (ABNB), Expedia (EXPE) and TripAdvisor (TRIP).

data.title: AstraZeneca's metastatic breast cancer treatment gets EU nod
data.description:
Daiichi Sankyo (TSE: 4568) and AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) said on Friday that Enhertu received European Union approval for the treatment of adults with advanced metastatic breast cancer.

Enhertu is a targeted HER2 therapy, approved now in the EU as a monotherapy to treat patients with advanced hormone receptor-positive breast cancer with low or ultra-low HER2 levels, who've had at least one endocrine therapy.

The approval follows the recommendation of the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use of the European Medicines Agency and results from late-stage trial showing 38% reduction in disease progression risk.

Enhertu is being jointly developed and commercialized by Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca.

"Following this approval in the EU, an amount of $125M is due from AstraZeneca to Daiichi Sankyo as a milestone payment for the HER2 low and HER2 ultralow chemotherapy-naive breast cancer indication," the companies stated.

ENHERTU is approved in over 75 countries, including the EU, for treating HER2-low metastatic breast cancer in patients who previously received systemic therapy or had a recurrence shortly after finishing chemotherapy.

data.title: SIMPPLE secures $524,000 contract
data.description:
• SIMPPLE (NASDAQ:SPPL) announced on Friday that the company has been awarded a follow-up contract, valued at $524,000, for the supply of autonomous cleaning robotics to Singapore’s international airport.
  • Shares of SPPL were up +4.69% to $0.424 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: INTC, DD, KHC and BKNG
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures slumped on Friday, as sentiment continued to be hammered by President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs announcement. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday: 

  • Shares of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) were -4.7% in pre-market trading, amid a broader slump in technology stocks sparked by tariffs turmoil. The Information on Thursday reported that INTC and the world's largest contract chipmaker, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), have reached a preliminary agreement to form a joint venture to operate some of Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) foundry facilities. 
  • DuPont (NYSE:DD) stock was -11.3% ahead of the opening bell. China opened a probe into the chemical and materials maker over alleged antitrust violations. According to China's State Administration for Market Regulation, Dupont China is suspected of violating the anti-monopoly law. DuPont (NYSE:DD) did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha. 
  • Shares of Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) were -1.5% ahead of market open. Citi downgraded the stock to Sell from Neutral. The brokerage sees risk to organic sales growth for the Kool-Aid and Jell-O-parent, and believes that Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) might "eventually need to reset its margin structure lower to drive volumes higher." KHC in February reported a FQ4 2024 fall in organic net sales. 
  • Booking Holdings (NASDAQ:BKNG) stock was -1.8% in pre-market trading. Still, in a positive development, BTIG upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral. The brokerage said tracking pointed to a FQ1 2025 beat for the online travel agency. BTIG also noted "visibility into a healthy" FQ2 was "building" and said that foreign exchange was putting upward pressure on BKNG's full-year numbers, "despite all of the macro malaise."

data.title: CleanSpark reports 13% higher bitcoin production in March
data.description:
• Bitcoin miner CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) announced on Friday the production of 706 bitcoins in March, up 13% sequentially.
  • Shares were trading -1.62% pre-market at $7.29.
  • The company's bitcoin holdings as of March 31 stood at 11,869, producing 1,956 counts of the cryptocurrency in 2025.
  • As of the month-end, operating hashrate was 42.4 EH/s.
  • CleanSpark sold 14.23 bitcoins in March.
  • "These tariffs may slow down competitors who lack our scale and resilience, but we're confident in our ability to maintain our momentum and lead the industry forward," said CEO Zach Bradford.
